mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, causing equity risk premiums to rise and S&P 500 returns to turn negative over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in retail sales and a deceleration in CPI inflation indicates strong real demand growth without triggering aggressive monetary tightening, creating a favorable risk-reward environment that drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint condition yields negative forward returns in >55% of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: During post-shock recovery phases, a month-over-month acceleration in the industrial production index reflects rapid capacity utilization and corporate earnings revisions, generating strong positive momentum in S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or the predictive alpha decays to zero within 3 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands household income and corporate revenue expectations, systematically supporting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive payroll growth correlates with negative 20-day returns in >60% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million units, it signals a sharp contraction in construction activity and consumer confidence, dragging down cyclical equity sectors and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are positive in >65% of periods where HOUST drops below 1200.0, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: High CPI inflation combined with a rising federal funds rate indicates a restrictive monetary policy response to price pressures, which compresses profit margins and equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint high-inflation/high-rate regime yields positive forward returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect is subsumed by a single-variable model with no loss in predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In early-cycle expansion regimes, a quarter-over-quarter increase in real GDP growth confirms broad-based economic acceleration, prompting institutional portfolio rebalancing into equities and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in >55% of early-cycle GDP expansion quarters, or the predictive relationship reverses during late-cycle phases without regime filtering.
